title: calystegia malacophylla subsp. tomentella
intro: Calystegia malacophylla subsp. tomentella, also known as the hairy woundwort or woolly bindweed, is a plant species in the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calystegia malacophylla subsp. tomentella include wound healing, skin infections treatment, and relief from fever and other symptoms. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
